properties.name: Donna Louise Apartments
properties.description: Family Housing community. "Family-friendly apartments in North Las Vegas providing affordable, comfortable living spaces with easy access to local services and amenities." Why Choose Donna Louise Apartments? Convenient North Las Vegas location: Donna Louise Apartments is located at 6225 Donna St. in ZIP code 89081, close to neighborhood schools, shopping, and everyday services. Built for family living: Public listing data describes the community as offering one, two, and three bedroom apartment homes, with the property built in 2017. Easy access around the area: Nearby bus stops along Donna Street and the Carey, Las Vegas Boulevard, and Lake Mead corridors provide local RTC connections for everyday travel. Around the neighborhood: Located in North Las Vegas, Donna Louise Apartments offers a practical residential setting with access to schools, parks, shopping, and transit connections throughout the area.
properties.tagline: Family-friendly apartments in North Las Vegas providing affordable, comfortable living spaces with easy access to local services and amenities.
amenities: One, two, and three bedroom apartment homes
Family community in North Las Vegas
Convenient access to shopping and daily essentials
Close to local parks and open space
properties.office_hours: Please call for current office hours and tour availability.
properties.ami_text: Availability and pricing subject to change. Income and program restrictions may apply.

issues: The page's only amenity list is the section titled 'Community features — What this community offers', which has exactly 8 items. The first pass captured all 8 correctly (no omissions).

The first pass added 3 entries that are NOT from the amenities list: 'Spacious homes: Thoughtfully designed floor plans', 'Family-friendly: Amenities that support daily life', and 'Professional management: On-site team & responsive service'. These are the page's hero 'highlights' strip, presented as heading + caption pairs (e.g. heading 'Spacious homes' with caption 'Thoughtfully designed floor plans') — generic marketing value-props, not amenities. They were reworded by joining each pair with a colon, which the page never does. Remove them from the amenity corpus.

Unit types: faithful. Page copy states 'One, two, and three bedroom apartment homes.' Note: the shared waitlist form additionally lists Studio/1BR/2BR/3BR as generic selectable options, but 'Studio' is NOT stated as an actual Donna Louise unit type in the listing copy, so it was correctly omitted.
Office hours: faithful. Page reads 'Hours: Please call for current office hours and tour availability.'
Pet policy: correctly null. The only 'Pets' reference on the page is a field label in the generic waitlist form; no pet policy is stated.

No traditional apartment-features/in-unit amenity block exists on the page (no AC, washer/dryer, etc.). Neighborhood POIs (e.g. U.S. Veterans Administration, NAACP Branch #1111, Buy Low Supermarket, Wells Fargo, Family Dollar) are 'around the neighborhood' points of interest, not property amenities, and were correctly excluded.

issues: No hallucinations: all 11 first-pass amenities verified present on the live page. A naive single fetch initially failed to surface the 3 'Why residents choose us' items ('Spacious homes', 'Family-friendly', 'Professional management'); a second targeted fetch confirmed they ARE on the page under the 'Why Choose Ethel Mae Fletcher Apartments?' section, so the first pass did NOT invent them.

Minor (non-breaking) reformatting: the 3 'Why residents choose us' items render on the page as a bold heading plus a separate one-line description; the first pass joined each as 'heading: description' with a colon. Content is preserved and not misleading.

Transparency note (not an amenity omission): the page also has a separate 'Neighborhood highlights / Around the neighborhood' marketing section with 3 location callouts — 'Convenient northwest location' (Easy access to shopping, schools, parks, and neighborhood services), 'Comfortable community living' (Designed for everyday convenience with access to nearby essentials), 'Well-connected neighborhood' (Close to key retail, recreation, and transit corridors in northwest Las Vegas). These are neighborhood-marketing taglines, not property amenities, so excluded from the amenities corpus.

Office hours on the page read 'Hours: Please call for current office hours and tour availability.'; first pass dropped the 'Hours:' label only — content matches. Pet policy is genuinely absent from the page (null is correct).

issues: HALLUCINATED/MISCLASSIFIED: First pass included 'Comfortable layouts & community spaces' as an amenity. It is NOT in the page's amenity list. It appears only as descriptive sub-copy under the hero highlight pair 'Designed for seniors'.

HALLUCINATED/MISCLASSIFIED: First pass included 'On-site team & responsive service' as an amenity. It is NOT in the page's amenity list. It appears only as descriptive sub-copy under the hero highlight pair 'Professional management'.

The page's sole amenity block is the section headed 'Community features' / 'What this community offers', which contains exactly 8 items. The first pass returned 10 by pulling two hero positioning blurbs into the list (while inconsistently omitting the matching third blurb 'Affordable housing programs'). All 8 genuine amenities were captured verbatim and worded correctly; no real amenities were missed.
MINOR (office hours): Page presents the line as 'Hours: Please call for current office hours and tour availability.' First pass dropped the 'Hours:' label but the substantive text matches.

NOTE (unit types): No dedicated floor-plans/unit-types section exists on the page. 'One-bedroom'/'Two-bedroom' is derived correctly from the community-features line 'One & two-bedroom senior apartments'. The waitlist form's Studio/1BR/2BR/3BR options are a shared site-wide form, not this property's inventory.
NOTE (pet policy): null is correct. The page states no pet policy; only a generic 'Pets' field exists in the shared waitlist form.

extraction_notes: Per-property amenities + details re-scraped live from gpmglv.com (the Global Property Management Group public site) per Craig's 2026-06-26 directive, so Frank can speak to each community's real amenities.

Content fields (amenities, description, unit_types, accessibility, pet_policy, office_hours, property_type) come from the live page; each property scraped then independently verified against the page (see per-record _verify).
Curated contact fields (phone, email, waitlist_url, address) preserved from the prior corpus; scrape only filled gaps (e.g. previously-missing leasing emails).
Locked grounding contract unchanged: rent_disclosed=false fleet-wide, available_units_count=null (status not count), AMI from statewide base only.
